<!-- GENERATED by tools/tasks/enumerate-port-rules.ts from tools/port-targets.json + the upstream/eslint-plugin-postgresql submodule. Do not edit by hand; run `pnpm run port:rules`. -->

# Port target: `eslint-plugin-postgresql`

| | |
|---|---|
| Upstream repo | https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql |
| Submodule | `upstream/eslint-plugin-postgresql` @ `v0.22.1` |
| Baseline npm version | `0.22.1` |
| License | MIT |
| Oxlint native support | none â€” port target |
| Rules to port | 89 |

> Lints SQL embedded in template literals; depends on the postgresql-eslint-parser port-target.

## Rules

| # | Rule | Description | Docs |
|---|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | `align-column-definitions` | Align column definitions vertically inside `CREATE TABLE` so that name, type, and constraints share consistent column offsets | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/align-column-definitions.md) |
| 2 | `align-values` | Align column values vertically inside multi-row `INSERT ... VALUES (...)` so that each tuple position shares a consistent width across rows | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/align-values.md) |
| 3 | `consistent-as-for-column-alias` | Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before column aliases in `SELECT` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-as-for-column-alias.md) |
| 4 | `consistent-as-for-table-alias` | Enforce a consistent stance on the `AS` keyword before table aliases (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-as-for-table-alias.md) |
| 5 | `consistent-between-over-and` | Enforce a consistent stance on `x BETWEEN a AND b` vs `x >= a AND x <= b` for closed-interval range checks | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-between-over-and.md) |
| 6 | `consistent-create-index-concurrently` | Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `CREATE INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-create-index-concurrently.md) |
| 7 | `consistent-create-or-replace` | Enforce a consistent stance on `CREATE OR REPLACE` for `FUNCTION` / `PROCEDURE` / `VIEW` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-create-or-replace.md) |
| 8 | `consistent-drop-index-concurrently` | Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `DROP INDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-drop-index-concurrently.md) |
| 9 | `consistent-explicit-inner-join` | Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `INNER` keyword in `INNER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-explicit-inner-join.md) |
| 10 | `consistent-explicit-outer-join` | Enforce a consistent stance on the explicit `OUTER` keyword in `LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER JOIN` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-explicit-outer-join.md) |
| 11 | `consistent-fk-not-valid` | Enforce a consistent stance on `NOT VALID` for `ALTER TABLE ... ADD FOREIGN KEY` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-fk-not-valid.md) |
| 12 | `consistent-identity-over-serial` | Enforce a consistent stance on `GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY` vs `SERIAL` / `BIGSERIAL` / `SMALLSERIAL` | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-identity-over-serial.md) |
| 13 | `consistent-jsonb-over-json` | Enforce a consistent stance on `jsonb` vs `json` for column types | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-jsonb-over-json.md) |
| 14 | `consistent-reindex-concurrently` | Enforce a consistent stance on `CONCURRENTLY` for `REINDEX` (either always require it, or always forbid it) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-reindex-concurrently.md) |
| 15 | `consistent-text-over-varchar` | Enforce a consistent stance on `text` vs `varchar(n)` for string columns | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-text-over-varchar.md) |
| 16 | `consistent-timestamptz` | Enforce a consistent stance on `timestamptz` vs `timestamp` (without time zone) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/consistent-timestamptz.md) |
| 17 | `no-add-check-constraint-without-not-valid` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK (...)` without `NOT VALID`; the synchronous form holds `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` on the table for the entire validating scan | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-add-check-constraint-without-not-valid.md) |
| 18 | `no-add-column-not-null-without-default` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL` without a `DEFAULT` because the migration fails outright on any non-empty table | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-add-column-not-null-without-default.md) |
| 19 | `no-add-unique-constraint-directly` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE (...)` written inline; build the index with `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` first, then promote it via `ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE USING INDEX <name>` | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-add-unique-constraint-directly.md) |
| 20 | `no-alter-column-type` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE ...` because it can rewrite the table under an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-alter-column-type.md) |
| 21 | `no-char-type` | Disallow the blank-padded `char(n)` / `bpchar` column type | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-char-type.md) |
| 22 | `no-cluster` | Disallow the `CLUSTER` statement: it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, rewrites the table, and is not maintained afterwards | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-cluster.md) |
| 23 | `no-composite-primary-key` | Disallow composite (multi-column) PRIMARY KEY constraints; use a single surrogate key and a UNIQUE constraint on the natural columns instead | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-composite-primary-key.md) |
| 24 | `no-create-role` | Disallow `CREATE ROLE` / `CREATE USER` in application migrations; manage roles in a separate operator workflow | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-create-role.md) |
| 25 | `no-cross-join` | Disallow `CROSS JOIN` (unqualified cartesian product) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-cross-join.md) |
| 26 | `no-distinct-on-without-order-by` | Disallow `SELECT DISTINCT ON (...)` without an `ORDER BY`; the surviving row in each group is otherwise non-deterministic | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-distinct-on-without-order-by.md) |
| 27 | `no-drop-column` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN` â€” every reader of the dropped column breaks at deploy time | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-drop-column.md) |
| 28 | `no-drop-database` | Disallow `DROP DATABASE`; it is catastrophic if run by accident and should not live in versioned SQL | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-drop-database.md) |
| 29 | `no-drop-not-null` | Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... DROP NOT NULL` â€” relaxing a NOT NULL constraint surprises every consumer that already assumes the column is non-null | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-drop-not-null.md) |
| 30 | `no-drop-schema-cascade` | Disallow `DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE`; it silently removes every object in the schema | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-drop-schema-cascade.md) |
| 31 | `no-drop-table-cascade` | Disallow `DROP TABLE ... CASCADE` because it silently removes dependent objects | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-drop-table-cascade.md) |
| 32 | `no-equality-with-null` | Disallow `x = NULL` / `x <> NULL`; PostgreSQL's three-valued logic makes both expressions evaluate to NULL (i.e. neither true nor false), which silently filters away rows the author probably wanted | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-equality-with-null.md) |
| 33 | `no-grant-all` | Disallow `GRANT ALL` / `GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES`; enumerate the privileges actually needed so the grant is auditable and a future PG release adding a new privilege does not silently extend it | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-grant-all.md) |
| 34 | `no-grant-to-public` | Disallow GRANT statements that target the `PUBLIC` pseudo-role | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-grant-to-public.md) |
| 35 | `no-group-by-ordinal` | Disallow `GROUP BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or expression instead | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-group-by-ordinal.md) |
| 36 | `no-having-without-group-by` | Disallow `HAVING` without `GROUP BY` â€” the query aggregates the entire result set, which is almost never the intended shape | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-having-without-group-by.md) |
| 37 | `no-identifier-too-long` | Disallow identifiers longer than PostgreSQL's `NAMEDATALEN - 1` limit (default 63 bytes) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-identifier-too-long.md) |
| 38 | `no-implicit-join` | Disallow comma-separated FROM clauses (implicit cross joins); use explicit JOIN syntax | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-implicit-join.md) |
| 39 | `no-leading-wildcard-like` | Disallow `LIKE`/`ILIKE` patterns that begin with `%` because they cannot use a B-tree index and force a full scan | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-leading-wildcard-like.md) |
| 40 | `no-money-type` | Disallow the `money` column type | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-money-type.md) |
| 41 | `no-natural-join` | Disallow `NATURAL JOIN` | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-natural-join.md) |
| 42 | `no-not-in-subquery` | Disallow `NOT IN (subquery)` because NULL values in the subquery silently return zero rows | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-not-in-subquery.md) |
| 43 | `no-numeric-without-precision` | Require an explicit precision (and scale) on `NUMERIC` / `DECIMAL` columns | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-numeric-without-precision.md) |
| 44 | `no-on-delete-cascade` | Disallow `ON DELETE CASCADE` on foreign keys; cascading deletes are easy to write but can wipe out far more rows than the author intended | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-on-delete-cascade.md) |
| 45 | `no-order-by-ordinal` | Disallow `ORDER BY <position>` (positional/ordinal references); use the column name or alias instead | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-order-by-ordinal.md) |
| 46 | `no-rename-column` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN` â€” every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-rename-column.md) |
| 47 | `no-rename-table` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO` â€” every deployed reader of the old name breaks at deploy time | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-rename-table.md) |
| 48 | `no-rule` | Disallow `CREATE RULE`; PostgreSQL's rule system is a known foot-gun and is effectively deprecated in favor of triggers and views | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-rule.md) |
| 49 | `no-security-definer-without-search-path` | Disallow `SECURITY DEFINER` functions that do not pin `search_path`; an attacker who controls a schema in the caller's `search_path` can shadow built-in objects and escalate privileges | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-security-definer-without-search-path.md) |
| 50 | `no-select-into` | Disallow `SELECT ... INTO target FROM ...` (creates a new table); use `CREATE TABLE AS SELECT ...` instead | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-select-into.md) |
| 51 | `no-select-star` | Disallow `SELECT *` in queries to keep result schemas stable when the underlying table changes | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-select-star.md) |
| 52 | `no-set-not-null` | Disallow `ALTER COLUMN ... SET NOT NULL` because it scans the whole table under ACCESS EXCLUSIVE | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-set-not-null.md) |
| 53 | `no-set-search-path` | Disallow `SET search_path = ...` in versioned SQL; qualify identifiers with their schema instead | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-set-search-path.md) |
| 54 | `no-syntax-error` | Disallow PostgreSQL syntax errors | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-syntax-error.md) |
| 55 | `no-temporary-table` | Disallow `CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE` in versioned SQL â€” temp tables exist for the session only and rarely belong in migration files | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-temporary-table.md) |
| 56 | `no-time-type` | Disallow `TIME` / `TIME WITH TIME ZONE` columns; they rarely model a real-world value correctly | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-time-type.md) |
| 57 | `no-truncate-cascade` | Disallow `TRUNCATE ... CASCADE` because it transitively empties referencing tables | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-truncate-cascade.md) |
| 58 | `no-unlogged-table` | Disallow `CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE` because unlogged tables are truncated on crash and not replicated | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-unlogged-table.md) |
| 59 | `no-unnecessary-quoted-identifier` | Disallow unnecessary double-quoting of identifiers (e.g. `"users"` when `users` would mean the same thing) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-unnecessary-quoted-identifier.md) |
| 60 | `no-update-primary-key` | Disallow `UPDATE ... SET <pk> = ...` for columns the rule treats as primary keys | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-update-primary-key.md) |
| 61 | `no-update-without-from-binding` | Disallow `UPDATE ... FROM other_table` without a `WHERE` clause; without a join condition the FROM table forms a Cartesian product with the target table and updates every row of the target as many times as `other_table` has rows | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-update-without-from-binding.md) |
| 62 | `no-vacuum-full` | Disallow `VACUUM FULL` because it takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE and rewrites the entire table | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-vacuum-full.md) |
| 63 | `no-volatile-default-on-add-column` | Disallow `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT <volatile>()`; volatile defaults force a full table rewrite under `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` because the stable-default short-cut cannot be used | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-volatile-default-on-add-column.md) |
| 64 | `no-with-recursive-without-limit` | Disallow `WITH RECURSIVE` queries that have no `LIMIT` on the outer SELECT, which can run unboundedly if the recursion's termination condition is wrong | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/no-with-recursive-without-limit.md) |
| 65 | `plpgsql-keyword-case` | Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL and PL/pgSQL keywords inside PL/pgSQL function bodies | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/plpgsql-keyword-case.md) |
| 66 | `prefer-add-constraint-not-valid` | Prefer `ADD CONSTRAINT ... NOT VALID` followed by a separate `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` to avoid an `ACCESS EXCLUSIVE` lock on the full table while it is being scanned | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-add-constraint-not-valid.md) |
| 67 | `prefer-bigint-id` | Prefer `bigint` for primary-key `id` columns; `int` / `smallint` primary keys risk silent overflow on growing tables | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-bigint-id.md) |
| 68 | `prefer-cast-operator` | Enforce a single style for type casts (`x::int` operator form vs `CAST(x AS int)` function form) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-cast-operator.md) |
| 69 | `prefer-coalesce-over-case` | Prefer `COALESCE(x, y)` over `CASE WHEN x IS NULL THEN y ELSE x END` (and its IS NOT NULL mirror) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-coalesce-over-case.md) |
| 70 | `prefer-current-timestamp-over-now` | Prefer SQL-standard `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` / `CURRENT_TIME` over PostgreSQL's `now()` and the timezone-naive `LOCALTIMESTAMP` / `LOCALTIME` | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-current-timestamp-over-now.md) |
| 71 | `prefer-exists-over-in-subquery` | Prefer `EXISTS (...)` over `... IN (subquery)` so NULL handling is unambiguous and the planner can use a semi-join | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-exists-over-in-subquery.md) |
| 72 | `prefer-explicit-null-ordering` | When `ORDER BY` specifies an explicit direction, require an explicit `NULLS FIRST` / `NULLS LAST` so null ordering is not implicit | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-explicit-null-ordering.md) |
| 73 | `prefer-in-list-over-or` | Prefer `x IN (a, b, c)` over a chain of `x = a OR x = b OR x = c` | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-in-list-over-or.md) |
| 74 | `prefer-keyword-case` | Enforce a consistent case (upper or lower) for SQL keywords | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-keyword-case.md) |
| 75 | `prefer-not-equals-operator` | Enforce a single style for the not-equal operator (`<>` or `!=`) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/prefer-not-equals-operator.md) |
| 76 | `require-fk-include-columns` | Require every foreign-key constraint to include a configured set of columns (e.g. `tenant_id` in a multi-tenant database) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-fk-include-columns.md) |
| 77 | `require-if-exists` | Require `IF EXISTS` on every `DROP` statement so re-running a migration on a database that already lost the object does not error | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-if-exists.md) |
| 78 | `require-index-on-fk-column` | Require an index on every foreign-key column; without it a `DELETE` or `UPDATE` on the referenced table sequentially scans the referencing table to enforce the constraint | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-index-on-fk-column.md) |
| 79 | `require-limit` | Require LIMIT clause in SELECT statements | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-limit.md) |
| 80 | `require-named-constraint` | Require an explicit name on table-level CHECK, UNIQUE, FOREIGN KEY, and EXCLUSION constraints | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-named-constraint.md) |
| 81 | `require-on-delete-action` | Require an explicit `ON DELETE` clause on every foreign-key constraint | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-on-delete-action.md) |
| 82 | `require-primary-key` | Require every `CREATE TABLE` to declare a primary key | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-primary-key.md) |
| 83 | `require-schema-qualified-table` | Require `CREATE TABLE` to use a schema-qualified name (e.g. `audit.events`) so the target schema is explicit | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-schema-qualified-table.md) |
| 84 | `require-table-columns` | Require every `CREATE TABLE` to include a configured set of columns (e.g. multi-tenant / audit columns like `tenant_id`, `created_at`, `updated_by`) | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-table-columns.md) |
| 85 | `require-trailing-semicolon` | Require a trailing `;` at the end of the SQL file | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-trailing-semicolon.md) |
| 86 | `require-where-in-delete` | Require a WHERE clause in DELETE statements | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-where-in-delete.md) |
| 87 | `require-where-in-update` | Require a WHERE clause in UPDATE statements | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/require-where-in-update.md) |
| 88 | `snake-case-column-name` | Require column names to be snake_case | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/snake-case-column-name.md) |
| 89 | `snake-case-table-name` | Require table names to be snake_case | [docs](https://github.com/baseballyama/eslint-plugin-postgresql/blob/main/docs/rules/snake-case-table-name.md) |
